Reader.java

00037 
00038 package java.io;
00039  
00040 /* Written using "Java Class Libraries", 2nd edition, plus online
00041  * API docs for JDK 1.2 beta from http://www.javasoft.com.
00042  * Status:  Believed complete and correct.
00043  */
00044 
00056 public abstract class Reader
00057 {
00063     protected Object lock;
00064   
00069     protected Reader()
00070     {
00071         this.lock = this;
00072     }
00073 
00080     protected Reader(Object lock)
00081     {
00082         this.lock = lock;
00083     }
00084 
00096     public int read() throws IOException
00097     {
00098         char[] buf = new char[1];
00099         int count = read(buf, 0, 1);
00100     
00101         return count > 0 ? buf[0] : -1;
00102     }
00103 
00121     public int read(char buf[]) throws IOException
00122     {
00123         return read(buf, 0, buf.length);
00124     }
00125 
00153     public abstract int read(char buf[], int offset, int count)
00154         throws IOException;
00155 
00174     public long skip(long count) throws IOException, IllegalArgumentException
00175     {
00176         if (count < 0)
00177             throw new IllegalArgumentException();
00178 
00179         int bsize = count > 1024 ? 1024 : (int) count;
00180         char[] buffer = new char[bsize];
00181         long todo = count;
00182 
00183         synchronized (lock)
00184         {
00185             while (todo > 0)
00186             {
00187                 int skipped = read(buffer, 0, bsize > todo ? (int) todo : bsize);
00188                 
00189                 if (skipped <= 0)
00190                     break;
00191                 
00192                 todo -= skipped;
00193             }
00194         }
00195 
00196         return count - todo;
00197     }
00198 
00211     public boolean ready() throws IOException
00212     {
00213         return false;
00214     }
00215 
00230     public boolean markSupported()
00231     {
00232         return false;
00233     }
00234 
00251     public void mark(int readLimit) throws IOException
00252     {
00253         throw new IOException("mark not supported");
00254     }
00255 
00266     public void reset() throws IOException
00267     {
00268         throw new IOException("reset not supported");
00269     }
00270 
00277     public abstract void close() throws IOException;
00278 }
